conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So, this is your first time seeing me. What brings you in today?
Yeah, I guess it is. Honestly, I've been having a really hard time lately. Just getting through the day feels like an accomplishment.
Can you tell me more about what's been going on that's making things so tough?
Well, I just started this new job as a registered nurse and it's...it's been overwhelming. There's so much to learn and remember, and my mind just feels like mush sometimes.
That sounds challenging. Have you noticed any changes in your daily routine or habits recently?
Yeah, definitely. I've lost interest in things I used to enjoy, like hiking with friends. Now it all feels like too much effort.
What about your social life? Are there any relationships that are causing you stress or concern?
Honestly, my social life is kind of non-existent right now. Work has been consuming me.
I see. How's your appetite been treating you lately?
It's been...noticeable. Like, I'll eat a whole pizza by myself because it's easier than cooking something else.
That sounds like it could be a challenge. Have you noticed any physical sensations or body feelings that might be related to this eating habit?
Yeah, sometimes my stomach hurts after eating that much. And I get these weird, fluttery feelings in my chest.
Can you tell me more about those physical sensations? Where do they feel like they're located?
It's mostly in my chest, but sometimes it spreads to my arms or legs. It's kind of...um...like a hummingbird, I guess. All fluttery and annoying.
That sounds uncomfortable. Have you experienced any recent stressful life events that might be contributing to these symptoms?
Yeah, there was this one patient who passed away on the ward, and it really hit me hard. I feel guilty for not being able to do more to help her.
I'm so sorry to hear that. It's natural to feel that way. How have you been coping with your emotions since then?
Honestly? Not very well. I just kind of go through the motions and try to distract myself from it all.
That sounds like a heavy burden to carry alone. Have you considered reaching out to anyone or any support system for help?
Yeah, I've been meaning to talk to my best friend about it, but...I don't know, it just feels so weird talking about this stuff with someone else.
It can be tough to open up to others. Is there anything you're looking forward to in the coming weeks or months that might help take your mind off things?
Actually, I'm supposed to go on a camping trip with some friends soon. That always helps me unwind and clear my head.
That sounds like a great way to recharge! What do you think is one thing we can work on in our sessions together that would help you feel more empowered or in control?
Hmm...I guess just being able to talk about this stuff openly without feeling judged or ashamed. That would be huge for me.
Absolutely. I'm here to listen and support you without judgment. We'll start by working on building that trust. How do you feel about our next session?
Yeah, it's fine. Thanks.
You're welcome. Remember, everything we discuss is confidential and safe.
Got it.
Before we wrap up today, are there any specific goals or changes you'd like to work towards in the coming weeks?
I guess just being able to enjoy things again without feeling like it's too much effort. And maybe not eating an entire pizza by myself every night.
Those sound like great goals! We'll work together to make progress on those. See you at our next session!
Yeah, okay. Thanks again.
You're welcome.
